desc:
The soil in this fertile valley allows the grass to grow tall and strong. The
unbroken horizon assures that this very landscape will continue for miles and
miles ahead. The sound of water can be heard to the west.
name: High Grass

desc:
As older grass withers and dries up, the new shoots higher and higher.
Entangling with each other, they create a thick curtain hiding the animals and
perhaps the occasional traveller from sight. The grass grows lower to the east,
and to the north a group of stately trees livens up the horizon.
name: High Grass

desc:
A cluster of low, but sturdy trees has settled on the plains. The oaks grow
apart from each other, allowing the sun to reach the lower plants and the
undergrowth. The ground is flat in all directions, and covered with grasses and
herbs.
name: Sparse Oaks

desc:
Lying east of the river Anduin, these grasslands grow in a quiet dignity. The
grass has a lustrous green colour and the land is smooth and gentle. In the
south an outcropping of trees dot the horizon, and in the northeast another
cluster of trees can be seen.
name: Grassland

desc:
The river Anduin lies to the west, its ample waters providing nutrients for this
gentle grassland. Tracks of different wild animals are imprinted in the soft
soil. These plains extend in all other directions with a gentle slope to the
north.
name: Grassland

desc:
The plains have risen and formed a small slope. The soil is fertile and the
vegetation is thriving and healthy. The most common plants are grasses of
several kinds, stretching about the landscape for many miles. Westward the flow
of the river Anduin can be heard.
name: Slope

desc:
The ground is slightly uneven with rocks scattered across the landscape.
Occasional clumps of flowers break the monotony of these plains. The grass is
very soft and flexes in the breeze. To the west the flow of the Anduin can be
heard but tall grass blocks the view.
name: Easy Slope

desc:
A solitary bed of flowers grows here, amongst the seemingly endless plains.
Tufts of weeds grow between the rows of flowers, and many plants are in state of
decay. The plains continue in all directions, rising slightly to the south.
name: Bed of Flowers

desc:
The field here is covered with short grass with some flowers growing in it. The
flowers have various colours ranging from yellow to blue. To the south one can
spot a bed of flowers and in the east one can see the plains stretch out till
the horizon.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Corn and different kinds of plants grow intermingled with the short dark green
grass that thrives here. The trees growing here are very high, casting a shadow
over the grass during the day. To the west the plains continue onwards to the
horizon.
name: Short Grass

desc:
A few trees with small plants growing below them grow in the short grass, that
has a light green colour. To the west the plains continue until they reach the
Anduin; in the far north is a small trail, and in the east is a clump of trees.
name: Short Grass

desc:
The short grass that grows here is a bit brownish. Some flowers grow in between
the grasses on the dark black soil. There is a trail to the north snaking
through the vast grasslands and one can see the plains stretching out to the
Anduin in the west.
name: Beside the Trail

desc:
This trail is mostly made up of small pebbles and little stones and is becoming
partly overgrown by weeds due to lack of use. The trail continues onwards to the
west heading for the Anduin. To the east, the trail begins to be made less out
of pebbles and more out of dirt.
name: On a Minor Trail

desc:
Some small vegetation like dandelions and buttercups have sprouted in between
the dominant grass that grows here. A few trees are growing scattered around
here, giving little refuge for small animals. To the south is a small trail,
which bends and continues onwards far into the east.
name: Plain beside a Trail

desc:
The plains in this area are covered by a lush green grass with beds of flowers
here and there in them. There are some elevations in the plains with trees on
them. To the northwest is a small hill with trees growing on top of it, and to
the west the plains stretch out to the great river Anduin.
name: Lush Green Grass

desc:
In the grasslands there are some trees with bushes growing beneath their
branches. The trees growing here are approximately sixteen feet tall. To the
east is a small hill with a group of trees on top of it. The plains in the west
continue onwards until they reach the river Anduin.
name: Grasslands near a Small Hill

desc:
The plains are covered by grass with a few trees scattered around. There are
some small rises and dips in the plains due to erosion from the heavy rainfall
in the past. In the lower spots, there is more vegetation. In the west the
plains spread out to the Anduin, to the north the plains become almost empty. A
small hill covered with trees can be seen to the southeast.
name: Plains

desc:
These plains are almost completely empty in comparison to the ones surrounding
them, save a few trees and weeds. There are stones of different sizes scattered
around the plains. To the south and the east the plains begin to fill up with
vegetation, to the west the plains continue onward to the Anduin, and in the
north they stretch out towards the foothills of the Grey Mountains.
name: Empty Plains

desc:
The ground here is very flat with only a few dips and rises in it. In these dips
grow some small bushes and different kinds of weeds. In the west the plains look
rather empty and in the north the tops of the Grey Mountains can barely be seen.
name: Plains

desc:
A few dandelions and some white flowers with yellow hearts can be found growing
among the grass. A few trees are standing solemnly around. In the far north the
plains stretch out to the foothills of the Grey Mountains.
name: Plains

desc:
The grass in this area is about two inches high, with small flowers growing
amongst it. There are some heaps on the ground supposedly made by some moles. A
few solemn trees grow in these plains, giving shelter for small animals. In the
north the peaks of the Grey Mountains can be seen. In the southeast one can see
the edge of a small forest, which stands out against the plains surrounding it.
name: Plains

desc:
Several kinds of herbs in different stages of growth grow amongst the grass. A
variety of different mosses flourish in this area, thriving in the wet soil.
There are some small groups of trees in the plains giving shelter and housing
for small animals. A small forest is visible to the south, breaking the monotony
of the continuous plains. In the far north the highest peaks of the Grey
Mountains can barely be seen.
name: Plains

desc:
The terrain swells a little, forming a very convenient place for a house. Indeed
there are some remains of an unspecified structure here, though almost burned to
the ground. A few charred beams stick up from the ground, wrapped with ivy-like
plants. Seedlings of trees growing among the ruins show that the fire must have
taken place many years ago.
name: Burned Ruins

desc:
The view to the south reveals decaying remains of a homestead. When examining
the ground, it appears to be partly burned and a few scattered trunks still lie
rotten on the ground as in memory of an old fire. Gnarled and black trees cover
the view to the west. Short grass covers the nutritious soil and leads on to the
north. The foliage grows higher to the northeast and east, continuing like that
all the way to the horizon.
name: Grass

desc:
The plains are rolling on and on. The grass tall, but rather small, and yet
nevertheless quite healthy and thick. The grass continues in all directions,
with greener and higher plants to the east, smaller and drier to the west.
name: Short Grass

desc:
An endless grassland extends in all directions, studded with clumps of taller
plants and teeming with life. As the animals are shy and wary, they cannot
always be seen, but the tracks and droppings testify that some animals have
passed here recently. A house, breaking the monotony of the plains, is
noticeable to the northeast.
name: Grassland

desc:
Two varieties of green grass cover the plain, the darker kind dominating the
northern parts. The wind shifts the grass softly, bringing a sweet fragrance
with it. Small animals make occasional sounds on their way through the grass,
but they remain unseen. The plains continue to the south and west, and a long
hedge is seen to the north. A row of plants, which flowers show a bluish colour
has been planted in the eastern direction. A house can be discerned behind it.
name: Grassland

desc:
Thorny shrubs have grown nearly three feet above the ground, forming a hedge.
Pink and white blossoms cover most of the hawthorns and red berries can be seen
on some. The undergrowth is richer near the thorns than in the rest of the
plain, indicating that someone has watered the bushes. The ground rises slightly
to the east and faint shape of a building can be seen to the southeast.
name: Hawthorns

desc:
The thorny shrubs rise three feet above the ground and have been planted to form
a long hedge. Some of the shrubs carry many small berries and they all carry
blossoms, mostly white but an occasional red blossom can be seen. The
undergrowth has grown rich near the hawthorns, which indicates that someone has
watered them. The shrubs grow to the west, east and south. Grasslands extend to
the north behind the hedge.
name: Hawthorns

desc:
Green and yellow grass covers the even ground, softly shifting in the occasional
wind. Flowers with golden stems growing here and there fill the air with a soft,
sweet scent. Every now and then there is a faint sound of some small animal
scurrying between the grasses. The plains continue in all directions but to the
south, where the planted thorns seem to form a hedge.
name: Grassland

desc:
Shrubs of different size and species grow amongst the medium long grass. One
might have some difficulty moving through the tall grass growing in this area.
To the north the length of the grass gets longer, but becomes slightly shorter
to the south.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
Only a few trees manage to grow here in the long grass, standing far apart from
each other. To the west and south one notices that the height of the grass
diminishes and to the north and east, the grass stretches out in a continuous
sea of green.
name: High Grass

desc:
The high grass makes it somewhat difficult to travel through. Small paths are in
the high grass, probably by large animals who have trampled the grass, in search
of water and food. To the west the grass gets shorter whereas all around here
the grass is somewhat the same length.
name: High Grass

desc:
The grass grows very high here and all a hunter could see of a deer in the
distant grass would be their antlers. Lone trees grow here and there, probably
sprouted before the grass was so long. To the north and west one can see the
height of the grass diminish, to the far north one can see the outlining of a
trail.
name: High Grass

desc:
The grass here is very high making movement through it somewhat difficult. The
grass gives good protection as a hiding place for small animals. Some trees grow
here and there, casting their shadow over the plain at day. To the south the
grass becomes taller and to the north is a small trail.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
The trail winds through the plains stretching to the east and west, covered by
small pebbles and tiny stones. Beside the trail outstretching grasslands are
visible, with a few trees and some wild corn growing in them. To the east the
trail can be seen turning to the north.
name: On a Minor Trail

desc:
The grass growing here is competing with the bushes and shrubs for the best
spots to sprout. The grass appears to win the competition, probably due to a low
supply of nutrients. To the south and east is the minor trail that runs through
the land between the Anduin and Mirkwood.
name: Near a Bend in the Minor Trail

desc:
The occasional tree can be found growing amongst the grass which covers the
ground here, including the elevations and depressions in the grasslands. To the
north the rim of a small forest can be seen, and to the south the outline of a
small trail can be seen.
name: Grasslands near a Small Forest

desc:
The forest floor is covered with a thin layer of humus. Various sizes of ferns
are growing where the sun shines on the forest floor. The oak trees are mostly
covered by toadstools and looking up, a few nests are visible within the
branches of the trees.
name: A Small Oak Forest

desc:
The grass here is some two inches high, with a few flowers growing amongst it.
On the ground are some small mounds made out of earth, supposedly made by moles.
Scattered around are some cedar and oak trees. The plains look like they stretch
out infinitely, except in the east where the rim of a small forest breaks the
line of the horizon.
name: Plains near a Small Forest

desc:
Flowers grow here in the vast grasslands, a welcome change to the grass which
covers the surrounding lands. A few trees are scattered around, standing
solemnly in the vast plain. The trees are beginning to grow more closely to each
other in the direction of the small forest in the northeast. To the far south is
a small trail, which makes travel easier through these vast plains.
name: Grasslands near a Small Forest

desc:
The meadow is fully covered by dark green grass, with different kinds of flowers
in it. A few small patches of the meadow are covered with bushes and shrubs,
providing a good refuge for small animals. To the south one can see a tiny trail
running through the grasslands.
name: In a Large Meadow

desc:
The trail is covered by a thin layer of gravel. A few branches of trees, that
grow beside the trail, are hanging over the trail. The trail continues west for
some distance and then begins to head south, while it continues in the east.
name: On a Minor Trail

desc:
Only a few trees grow here, with some big shrubs and bushes growing in small
patches. To the west and north is a minor trail and in the northwest a bend can
be seen in it. In the southeast the grass grows higher.
name: Grasslands besides the Trail

desc:
In the grass grow a variety of plants, all barely sticking their heads over the
grass. To the east the grass becomes even higher, whereas it becomes shorter in
the west. In the northwest one can see a bend in a small trail.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
The grass that grows here is taller then in the west, whereas it is shorter then
the grass that grows in the east. In between the grass grows some dandelions and
some buttercups. In the west the grass is much shorter and one can see bushes
growing in it. In the east the grass begins to grow taller and very thick.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
Shrubs and bushes of different sizes grow on the dark brownish soil in between
the tall grass. To the west one can barely see the outline of a structure of
some kind in the short grass. In the east the grass becomes higher.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
Only a few flowers manage to grow tall enough to stretch their heads above the
high grass that grows here. To the northeast the grass grows higher, whereas the
grass grows shorter in the west and south.
name: Medium High Grass

desc:
The plain is covered with several sorts of grass. The darker kind grows slightly
higher and dominates the northern as well as the eastern parts of the plain.
Occasional dandelions are seen wherever the grass has failed to reach more than
a few inches in height. The plain continues in all directions.
name: Grass

desc:
Thorny shrubs with white and red blossoms and small red berries seem to have
been planted here to form a hedge. The hawthorns rise nearly three feet above
the ground and continue in a fairly straight line to the east. Grass-covered
plains continue in the other directions and a slope can be seen to the
southwest.
name: Hawthorns

desc:
The dark grass stands almost two feet high and covers most of the ground,
preventing the undergrowth to thrive. A few reddish flowers grow beside a large
stone, around which the soil is dark and no grass grows. Thorns can be seen to
the north and northeast, likely planted to form a hedge. The ground rises
slightly to the west leading up to a slope.
name: Grassy Corner

desc:
The grass is lustrously green and flowers of different colours have taken root
amongst the hardy grass of this region. The ground rises quickly to the west,
leading up to the top of a slope, behind which the colourful crowns of many
trees can be seen. The shape of something that could possibly be a house, can be
spotted far to the east.
name: Grassland

desc:
In the midst of these flat grasslands a patch of flowers springs to life. The
stems are thick and green. The flowers, when they can be seen, are small, white
and yellow. Grassland stretch from the southeast to the north. The ground rises
to the northwest.
name: Flowery Patch

desc:
The plants on this stretch of the steppe are in dire need for nutrition: they
look small, dry and withered. The ground is yellowish, and the soil seems to
contain lots of sand. To the south stands a group of small, twisted trees.
Flowers of different colours cover the ground to the north.
name: Short Dry Grass

desc:
The trees here have undergone some unnatural change: they are low, gnarled and
black. Their trunks are scarred, and their lower branches seem to have been
clipped. This effect is most evident to the south. The northern parts of this
coppice appear much healthier. Short grass grows to the north, along with green
crowns of high trees.
name: Gnarled Trees

desc:
Sparsely distributed trees form a small coppice here. The trees have a strange
quality about them: the trunks are black, and all their lower branches are gone.
Black coals lay on the ground, covered however by new foliage.
name: Scorched Trees

desc:
Open plains covered with grass - such is the landscape here. As far as one can
see, the same kind of environment stretches all the way from southwest to north.
Among the grass to the south, a huge lonely rock can be spotted. Dark, scattered
trees grow to the east.
name: Grass

desc:
The grass is low and weak, very distinct from the lush plains to the southwest.
The northern parts are visibly sloping down, and are even more scattered,
covered just with small clumps of grasses amidst pebbles and gravel. Low, dark
line of trees meets the eye looking to the east.
name: Grass

desc:
The ground forms a slight depression here. It is filled with sand, gravel and
pebbles. It is not quite a desert, plants are still growing here. It is just
that the grass is sparse, low and delicate with long stretches of sandy ground
in between.
name: Sandy Depression

desc:
The rocks and pebbles covering the ground hinder the growth of the grass. The
area is sparsely covered with low, spiny bushes that are almost devoid of leaves
and flowers. Their twigs are mostly dried and sharp, making it easy to prick
one's skin here. To the south is a patch of smaller gravel and sand. To the
north looms a low, dark ridge across the plains.
name: Dried Twigs and Pebbles

desc:
You find yourself on the top of a slope where the grass bends slavishly with the
breeze. It seems as if some animals have been here repeatedly, leaving a small
trail of beaten grass leading off to the south. Tall oaks, proudly stretching up
towards the sky, can be seen to the west.
name: Crest

desc:
The vegetation is rich here. Flowers bloom in clumps beside the green grass,
giving the ground a pale yellow colour. A soft breeze brings a rather faint but
sweet scent from these flowers. The ground rises further to the west towards the
top of the slope. The grass has grown taller to the east whilst shorter in other
directions.
name: Easy Slope

desc:
The ground has been torn up in many spots, roots of several plants exposed above
the earth. Large clumps of dirt dot the horizon, hardened from the exposure to
the sun. The ground rises slightly to the south and the west.
name: Trampled Grass

desc:
Various grasses dominate over this gentle plain, most appearing in green colour.
Grass tips, golden like honey, grow taller and give off a sweet scent.
Occasional droppings of animals can be seen in the short grass, indicating an
active wild life. The plains continue in all directions and the ground rises
slightly in the southwest.
name: Short, Diverse Grass

desc:
Small bushes are flourishing in the grass, with dark green leaves. A few trees
are also growing here, standing tall in the field. To the east one can see the
grasslands covered by taller grasses than in this area.
name: Short Grass

desc:
In the short grass one can see the remains of a small hut that looks as if it
had burned to the ground a long time ago. All that remains now is some of the
structure. Near it is an old well that has collapsed. To the east the grass
grows steadily longer, while it is shoulder-high in the far east.
name: At a Razed Hut

desc:
The grassland has many bushes of various sizes growing in it. The bushes are
mostly growing together in small groups. To the west one can see a big group of
trees and in the east the grass becomes taller.
name: Bush Filled Grassland

desc:
Flowers of different colours and sizes grow in the short dark-green grass. Some
trees also grow here on small rises in the plains. To the north one can see a
trail and in the east the grass is much longer. A clump of trees is to the
southwest.
name: Grasslands near a Trail

desc:
The trail is a small strip of land that is made up out of gravel and small
pebbles. Here the trail bends around several large rocks, abandoning its
east-to-west course. Beside the road lies grassland with an occasional tree and
some shrubs in it.
name: Bend in the Minor Trail

desc:
The trail is covered with gravel and pebbles making travel easier through the
vast plains. The grassland beside the road has some wild flower beds in it and
some trees with weeds growing beneath them. The trail continues onwards in the
south and east.
name: Bend in the Minor Trail

desc:
Buttercups and other small flowers grow in the grass covered plain. Here and
there an occasional tree has sprouted up in the rich soil. To the south and the
far east a trail can be seen stretching out into the west and the east. To the
far northwest is a small hill with a large number of trees on it.
name: Grasslands besides a Trail

desc:
The grass, in which flowers are growing, is only a few inches long. Here and
there grows an occasional tree. To the west a hill can be spotted with a lot of
trees on it. Far in the south a small trail can barely be seen lying in the vast
plains.
name: Grasslands

desc:
There are some flowerbeds in this field. There is also some wild corn growing in
patches. To the southwest a small hill can be seen, one of the few places that
stands out in the seemingly endless plains.
name: In a Field

desc:
There are some groups of trees scattered all across the plains with some weeds
and bushes growing beneath them. To the south a small hill can be seen with a
copse of trees on it. In the northwest the plains are getting empty, with only a
few trees in them.
name: Plains

desc:
A group of trees grows on top of this hill and small bushes and weeds grow at
the foot of the hill itself. The hills break up the monotony of the
outstretching grasslands. Far in the northeast, the edge of a small forest can
barely be seen and a minor trail can be spotted in the distant south.
name: On Top of a Small Hill

desc:
In the grass covered plains grow some small shrubs and bushes besides the trees
for shadow and protection. To the north are a lot of trees on a small hill, to
the far south and southeast is a small trail lying in the plains heading west
and eastwards.
name: Near a Small Hill

desc:
In between the grass, buttercups and other small flowers have seen a chance to
sprout up. Trees grow all over, but with quite some distance between them. In
the east and south is a trail which would make an easier way of transportation
through the vast plains.
name: Plain beside a Trail

desc:
The trail in this area is made up of pebbles and gravel but begins to become
almost completely dirt farther to the west. Bushes and trees are growing beside
the trail. In the east the trail continues onwards to a bend and in the west it
continues its journey towards the Anduin.
name: On a Minor Trail

desc:
The grass growing here is extremely short, implying that some herbivores have
passed here recently. There are some fruit trees growing here with their
branches waving in the wind. To the north is a small trail, which also can be
seen in the northwest and northeast, to the south is a clump of trees.
name: Short Grass

desc:
In the grass is a clump of trees with shrubs and bushes growing below and around
the clump itself. The grass growing around the clump is short and has a healthy
green colour. To the east the grass becomes longer and to the far north one can
barely see a small trail.
name: Clump of Trees

desc:
The flowers that grow here in the short grass are of various colours and sizes.
The trees in this area are all around thirteen feet tall. To the north one can
see a clump of trees and in the east one can see a vague black structure
standing in the middle of the plain.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Flowers of different colours grow among the short grass. Some small bushes with
bright green leaves are growing beneath the few trees in this area. All around
one can only see extending grassland with short grass and some trees growing in
it.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Tiny insects dart back and forth between the short grass. A fresh scent
overwhelms your senses as it drifts up from chewed or trampled stems of plants.
A large number of deer tracks criss-cross the fertile ground.
name: Short Grass

desc:
Blades of golden grass grow thick and tall. Animal droppings are scattered
about, and broken stems of grass attest to the existence of some large grazers
nearby. The ground rises gently to the west and south. The plains continue to
the north.
name: Easy Slope

desc:
Here the ground is slightly higher than the fields surrounding it. Remnants of a
past campfire are still visible among the grass, pieces of coal; white and
brittle. The ground declines slightly in all directions, and a cluster of trees
growing to the south attracts attention.
name: Slope

desc:
Tall oaks have claimed their spot in these vast plains. The space between the
trees as well as their stately appearance give the area a very groomed look. The
ground rises slightly to the north and the ascent is even steeper in the east,
where a ridge in the plains can be seen.
name: Cluster of Trees

desc:
Golden grass grows high on this vast plain. Animal tracks lay scattered about
the terrain, leaving signs of movement onto the grass. Although a dandelion or
two bloom in the thick grass, nothing else grows here. To the north a tall stand
of trees can be seen towering towards the sky.
name: South of a Tall Outcrop

desc:
There is a group of oak trees to the west, easily detected on the flat horizon
created by these grassy plains. Delicate flowers peek through the bold grass,
which is thick and strong. A cluster of what looks like dandelions can be
spotted to the south.
name: Grass

desc:
Amid the grasses, another plant has won a piece of land. The seeds must have
floated in on the wind and taken root. Now the whole area is covered with
dandelions - yellow in spring, white in the fall, but green all year round.
name: Dandelions

desc:
Deer tracks cover the ground, as well as other signs of animal life. The grass
grows thick and tall, providing enough coverage for a large animal to hide in
it. A large rock, rising high above the rather flat plains, is seen to the
southeast. The plains extend in all directions and the grass grows exceptionally
high to the west and northwest.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
A few wild plants of different colours thrive in the dark soil together with
large groups of dandelions and lilies. A grey stone lies defying the wind while
the vegetation shifts softly in its direction. Ivy covers most of the wall to
the west but a wooden door can still be seen beneath.
name: Backyard

desc:
There are many sorts of colourful flowers thriving peacefully on the ground.
Small stones are placed in two rows around a tiny trail, leading towards the
door of a house which stands in the east. Bushes which foliage is of a bluish
colour, have been planted in three rows, connecting with the two corners of the
house's wall. This gives you the impression of a well-tended garden in which the
vegetation is carefully and thoroughly framed.
name: Lead-in to a House

desc:
This is a small room with nothing mentionable but an old wooden bed. The air is
rather dusty and thick. The stench of moist and decay fills the room. The
occasional small animal indicates that it has been a while since the owner did
any maintenance work here. This place is likely designed for a quick escape from
unwanted visitors. The only way out seems to be by walking up the flight of
stairs to get through the hatch above.
name: Hiding Room

desc:
The walls look very smooth, giving the impression that this cave isn't totally
natural. The ceiling of the cave has some stalactites hanging down from it and
on the floor are some stalagmites, made by the soft dripping of water from the
ceiling. There are some black mounds lying around in the cave.
name: In the Hill

desc:
There are some thick branches in this tree which look sturdy enough to support
the weight of two persons easily. The canopy of the tree is several feet higher,
blocking out the sun's rays almost fully on the branches in the tree.
name: On Some Thick Branches in an Oak Tree

desc:
You find yourself at the top of the outcrop. The view from this spot extends for
many miles around. On the west, the river Anduin can barely be discerned. Far to
the east, running from north to south, the dark line of Mirkwood marks the
boundaries of the grasslands. Closer at hand, a strange circle of small stones
and dead branches has been built in a crack of the rock.
name: Peak of the Rock

desc:
You find yourself in a furnished room with only the most necessary facilities. A
bed stands close to one of the corners. Next to it is an old bedside table. A
table and chair are placed close to the bed beneath a small round window. The
house is built of a dark wood that looks very solid. A stone fireplace stands in
another corner and beside it lies a heap of bright firewood. A small square on
the floor in front of the bed is made of a slightly brighter wood.
name: Inside a House
